name: Marcus Curtius
description: Marcus Curtius was a Roman hero renowned for his self-sacrifice and bravery. According to legend, in 362 BC, a massive chasm opened in the Roman Forum, and the soothsayers declared that it would only close if Rome's greatest treasure was thrown into it. Some suggested material wealth, but Curtius believed Rome's greatest treasure was its citizens' courage and military prowess. Dressed in full armor and riding his warhorse, he declared that no Roman possession was more valuable than a brave soldier and plunged into the abyss. The chasm miraculously closed, and Curtius became a symbol of Roman patriotism and selflessness, embodying the virtues of courage, duty, and devotion to the state. His act solidified his place in Roman legend, and the Lacus Curtius in the Roman Forum was named in his honor, serving as a constant reminder of his sacrifice and the importance of putting the republic before oneself. He stands as a testament to the values Romans held dear and continues to inspire acts of courage and selflessness.
subtitle: Roman hero of self-sacrifice
achievements: Closed the chasm in the Forum
Became a symbol of Roman patriotism

keyMyths.title: The Chasm in the Forum
keyMyths.description: A deep chasm opened in the Roman Forum, threatening the city's stability. Soothsayers prophesied that Rome's greatest treasure needed to be sacrificed to close it.

keyMyths.title: Curtius' Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: Marcus Curtius, believing Rome's greatest treasure was its valor, rode his horse into the chasm, sacrificing himself for the city's safety. The chasm closed immediately after.
keyMyths.source: Valerius Maximus, Memorable Deeds and Sayings

keyMyths.title: The Lacus Curtius
keyMyths.description: The site where Curtius plunged into the abyss became known as the Lacus Curtius, a lasting monument to his sacrifice and Roman heroism. It served as a reminder of his devotion.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: Provides a detailed account of the chasm and the prophecy.
sources.text: Provides a detailed account of the chasm and the prophecy.

sources.author: Valerius Maximus
sources.description: Offers an alternative perspective on the sacrifice and its meaning.
sources.text: Offers an alternative perspective on the sacrifice and its meaning.
